title: HEP-TILE: HBV Whole Genome Sequencing (Nanopore Protocol)
author: Sheila Lumley

steps.instruction: Set up the two PCR reactions per sample as follows in strip-tubes or plates. Gently mix by pipetting and pulse spin the tube to collect liquid at the bottom of the tube. Samples > 5log10 IU/ml A B C 1 Component Reaction 1 Reaction 2 2 Q5 Hot Start High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix 12.5 µL 12.5 µL 3 V3 Pool 1 (10µM) 2.5 µL 0 µL 4 V3 Pool 2 (10µM) 0 µL 2.5 µL 5 Nuclease-free water 7.5 µL 7.5 µL 6 (Sample) (2.5ul) (2.5ul) 7 Total 25 µL 25 µL Samples 5 log10 IU/ml use 2.5ul < 5 log10 IU/ml use 7ul PCR reactions can be performed in duplicate for low viral load samples e.g. < 3 log10 IU/ml To prevent pre-PCR contamination the mastermix for each pool should be made up in the mastermix cabinet which should should be cleaned with decontamination wipes and UV sterilised before and after use and aliquoted into PCR strip-tubes/plate

substeps.instruction: Set up the two PCR reactions per sample as follows in strip-tubes or plates. Gently mix by pipetting and pulse spin the tube to collect liquid at the bottom of the tube. Samples > 5log10 IU/ml A B C 1 Component Reaction 1 Reaction 2 2 Q5 Hot Start High-Fidelity 2X Master Mix

µL 6 (Sample) (2.5ul) (2.5ul) 7 Total 25 µL 25 µL Samples 5 log10 IU/ml use 2.5ul < 5 log10 IU/ml use 7ul PCR reactions can be performed in duplicate for low viral load samples e.g. < 3 log10 IU/ml To prevent pre-PCR contamination the mastermix for each pool should be made up in the mastermix cabinet which should should be cleaned with decontamination wipes and UV sterilised before and after use and aliquoted into PCR strip-tubes/plate

steps.instruction: Add DNA to each of the PCR reactions (volume determined by VL of sample, see step 5), gently mix by pipetting and pulse spin the tube to collect liquid at the bottom of the tube. DNA should be added in the DNA/sample addition cabinet which should should be cleaned with decontamination wipes and UV sterilised before and after use.

Set-up the following program on the thermal cycler, use 35 cycles if VL > 5 log10 IU/ml, use 40 cycles if VL < 5 log10 IU/ml. Step Temperature Time Cycles Heat Activation 98 °C 00:00:30 1 Denaturation 98 °C 00:00:15 35 or 40 Annealing 65 °C 00:05:00 35 or 40 Hold 4 °C Indefinite 1

Label strip-tubes/plate and combine the following volumes of each PCR reaction for 25 µL each sample: A B 1 Component Volume 2 Pool 1 PCR reaction 25 μL 3 Pool 2 PCR reaction 25 µL 4 Total 50 µL Amplicons should be added in the post-PCR cabinet which should should be cleaned with decontamination wipes and UV sterilised before and after use.
In a new PCR strip-tube/plate set up the following reaction for each sample, normalising to 80ng amplicon DNA input per sample. Add an additional reagent-only negative control at this step.

Quantify 1 µL of the barcoded amplicons using a fluorometer such as a Qubit or Quantus. Concentration will vary depending on number and Ct of samples and but you need about 30 ng total at this stage to achieve maximum run yield.

In a new 1.5ml eppendorf or PCR tube set up the following adapter ligation reaction A B 1 Component Volume 2 Barcoded amplicon pool 30 ul 3 NEBNext quick ligation reaction buffer (5X) 10 ul 4 Adaptor mix (NA) 5 ul 5 Quick T4 DNA ligase 5 ul 6 Total 50 ul
Thoroughly mix the contents of the library beads by pipetting, it is vial that they are mixed immediately before use. From experience we have successfully loaded 20ng library
If making up primer pools from individual oligos fully resuspend lyophilised oligos in 1xTE to a concentration of 100 micromolar (µM) , vortex thoroughly and spin down.

Dilute 100 micromolar (µM) pools 1:10 in molecular grade water, to generate 10 micromolar (µM) primer stocks. Primers are used at a final concentration of 15 nanomolar (nM) per primer. In this case hbv/600/v2.1.0 pools have 69 primers in pool 1 and 63 primers in pool 2. so the requirement is ~ 2.5 µL primer pool ( 10 micromolar (µM) ) per 25 µL reaction. Make up multiple 100 µL aliquots of 10 micromolar (µM) primer dilutions and freeze them in case of degradation or contamination.

Sort all odd regions primers into one or more tube racks. Add 5 µL of each odd region primer to a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube labelled "Pool 1 ( 100 micromolar (µM) )". Repeat the process for all even region primers for Pool 2. These are your 100 micromolar (µM) stocks of each primer pool. Primers should be diluted and pooled in the mastermix cabinet which should be cleaned with decontamination wipes and UV sterilised before and after use. For more information see Figure 2 in; Quick, J. et al. Multiplex PCR method for MinION and Illumina sequencing of Zika and other virus genomes directly from clinical samples. Nat Protoc 12, 1261–1276 (2017). https://doi.org/10.1038/nprot.2017.066

Prepare between 11 and 94 DNA samples plus 1 negative control of nuclease-free water per library (a 2nd negative control is added at the start of library prep). Batch samples by viral load, processing samples above/below 5 log10 IU/ml separately. If previously frozen, mix by briefly vortexing and pulse spin to collect liquid. Keep samples on ice at all times. A positive control can also be included which may be a the NIBSC HBV control or clinical sample. This can help monitor run performance.
Add 40 µL SPRI beads to 50 µL pooled PCR product for an 0.8x clean up. Gently mix and incubate for 00:05:00 at room temperature
Place on a magnetic rack and incubate for 00:02:00 or until the beads have pelletted and supernatant is completely clear. Remove and discard the supernatant.
Perform an ethanol wash: add 100 µL of room temperature 70% ethanol to bathe the pellet, carefully remove and discard ethanol being careful not to touch the bead pellet.
Repeat the ethanol wash (step 9.2)
Pulse centrifuge to collect and remove residual ethanol, remove excess with a P10 pipette, it is very important all is removed.
With the tube lid open, air dry for 1-2 minutes, or until the pellet loses its shine. Do not over-dry.
Re-suspend pellet in 20 µL H20, mix gently and incubate for 2 minutes at room temperature
Place on magetic rack until clear, transfer the supernatant to clean labelled tubes, do not disturb the beads.
Quantify using a Qubit or Quantus.

A B 1 Component Volume 2 Clean PCR product from previous step Up to 8.3 ul 3 Ultra II end prep reaction buffer 1.2ul 4 Ultra II end prep enzyme mix 0.5ul 5 Nuclease-free water to a total of 10ul 6 Total 10ul Make a master mix of end-preparation reagents and nuclease-free water and aliquot into strip-tube/plate to improve reproducability.
Incubate at room temperature for 00:15:00 Incubate at 65 °C for 00:15:00 Incubate on ice for 00:01:00
In a new PCR strip-tube/plate set up the following reaction for each sample:

A B 1 Component Volume 2 End-preparation reaction mixture 1 ul 3 NBXX barcode 1.25 ul 4 Blunt/TA Ligase master mix 5 ul 5 Nuclease-free water 2.75 ul 6 Total 10 ul Use one native barcode per sample. Use 12 or more barcodes per library or there will be insufficient total material to achieve good yields.
Incubate at room temperature for 00:20:00

Add EDTA and mix thoroughly A B 1 EDTA cap colour Volume per well 2 For clear cap EDTA 1ul 3 For blue cap EDTA 2ul NB. EDTA concentration varies by cap colour supplied by ONT Alternatively a 65C heat step can be used to inactivate the ligase - see ONT version of protocol.

In a new 1.5ml Eppendorf tube pull all one-pot barcoding reactions together. If processing 12-24 samples pool all 10 µL from each native barcoding reaction. if processing 48 samples pool 5 µL from each native barcoding reaction. If processing 96 samples pool 2.5 µL from each native barcoding reaction so as not to exceed a pool volume of 240 µL which would make the clean-up volume too large.

Add ampure beads (supplied with ONT kit) for a 1.5x clean up. If very large volume split between 2 tubes Example: A B 1 Component Example volumes 2 Pooled barcoded samples 240 ul 3 Water 240 ul 4 Beads 720 ul NB. 0.4x clean up in ONT protocol was leading to loss of whole library.
Determine volume of pooled barcoded samples, add an equal volume of water
Mix by vortexing and pulse centrifuge to collect all liquid at the bottom of the tube. Incubate for 00:05:00 at room temperature.

Place on magnetic rack and incubate for 00:02:00 or until the beads have pelleted and the supernatant is completely clear. Carefully remove and discard the supernatant, being careful not to touch the bead pellet.
Add 250 µL SFB and resuspend beads completely by pipette mixing. Pulse centrifuge to collect all liquid at the bottom of the tube and place on the magnet. Remove supernatant and discard.
Repeat step 11.9 to perform a second SFB wash. Pulse centrifuge and remove any residual SFB. You do not need to allow to air dry with SFB washes.
Add 200 µL of room-temperature 70 % ethanol to bathe the pellet. Carefully remove and discard ethanol, being careful not to touch the bead pellet. Only perform 1x 70% ethanol wash
Pulse centrifuge to collect all liquid at the bottom of the tube and carefully remove as much residual ethanol as possible using a P10 pipette.

With the tube lid open incubate for 00:03:00 or until the pellet loses it's shine (very large pellets will take longer, NB if the pellet dries completely it will crack and become difficult to resuspend).

00:02:00 Re-suspend pellet in 31 µL nuclease free water, mix gently by either flicking or pipetting and incubate for 00:02:00 . If beads were split between two eppendorfs then combine at this point to elute in a total of 31ul.
Place on magnet and transfer sample to a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube ensuring no beads are transferred into this tube.
Set up the following Native Adapter (NA) ligation and clean-up with SFB.
Incubate at room temperature for 00:20:00
Perform a bead clean up.
Add 50ul ampure beads (supplied with ONT kit) to the sample tube. Mix by vortexing and pulse centrifuge to collect all liquid at the bottom of the tube. Incubate for 00:05:00 at room temperature.

Place on magnetic rack and incubate for 00:02:00 or until the beads have pelleted and the supernatant is completely clear. Carefully remove and discard the supernatant, being careful not to touch the bead pellet.
Add 250 µL SFB and resuspend beads completely by pipette mixing. Pulse centrifuge to collect all liquid at the bottom of the tube and place on the magnet. Remove supernatant and discard.
Repeat step 15.3 to perform a second SFB wash. Pulse centrifuge and remove any residual SFB. You do not need to allow to air dry with SFB washes.
Re-suspend pellet in 15 µL EB (ONT), mix gently by either flicking or pipetting and incubate for 00:02:00
Place on magnet and transfer sample to a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube ensuring no beads are transferred into this tube.
Refer to ONT documentation for images explaining how to check, prime and load flow cell.

Complete a flow cell check - open Minknow user interface, plug in MinION to laptop, open the MinION lid and slide the flow cell under the clip. Press down firmly on the flow cell to ensure correct thermal and electrical contact. Click flow cell check on Minknow interface.

To prepare the flow cell priming mix, combine the following reagents in the FCF tube and mix by inverting the tube and pipette mix at room temperature: A B 1 Component Volume 2 Flow cell flush (FCF) 1170 ul 3 Bovine serum albumin (BSA) at 50mg/ml 5 ul 4 Flow cell tether (FCT) 30 ul 5 Final volume in FCF tube 1205 ul
Slide the flow cell priming port cover clockwise to open the priming port

After opening the priming port, set a P1000 pipette to 200ul, insert the tip into the priming port, turn the wheel until the dial shows 220-230ul, to draw back 20-30ul or until you can see a small volume of buffer entering the pipette tip.
Visually check that there is continuous buffer from the priming port across the sensor array.

Load 800 µL of the priming mix into the flow cell via the priming port, avoiding the introduction of air bubbles. Wait for five minutes. During this time prepare the library for loading by following the steps below.

In a new 1.5ml Eppendorf DNA LoBind tube, prepare the library for loading as follows (NB load the library onto flow cell immediately after adding SB and LIB because the fuel in the buffer will start to be consumed by the adapter): A B 1 Reagent Volume 2 Sequencing buffer (SB) 37.5 ul 3 Library beads (LIB) mixed immediately before use 25.5 ul 4 DNA library + H2O 12 ul 5 Total 75 ul
Complete flow cell priming: lift the spot on sample port cover, load 200 µL of the priming mix into the flow cell priming port (not the SpotON sample port), avoiding the introduction of air bubbles
Mix the prepared library gently by pipetting just prior to loading

Add half of the library (~37.5ul) to the flow cell via the SpotON sample port in a dropwise fashion. Ensure each drop flows into the port before adding the next. Resuspend beads/library and add the 2nd half in a dropwise fashion.
Gently replace the SpotON sample port cover, making sure the bung enters the SpotON port and close the priming port.
Place the light shield onto the flow cell. And close the device lid.

Quantify the final library using a fluorometer such as a Qubit or Quatus. Concentration will vary depending on number and Ct of samples, expected quantification between 2-9 ng/ul, 15 ng final library is usually required to acheive maximum run yield. Final library can be now be stored at 4 °C for up to a week if needed otherwise proceed directly to MinION sequencing.
Start the sequencing run using MinKnow. If using live basecalling, turn on double-ended barcoding in the basecalling settings.
